refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable monitoring, controlling, and optimizing energy consumption to meet the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad; decomposing it into monitoring, control, and optimization clarifies how energy efficiency can be achieved and verified.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify by measuring energy_per_ton over the evaluation period and confirming it meets ≤ E_target; validate monitoring data accuracy, control actions, and optimization effectiveness.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved in real-time.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize operational parameters and task scheduling to improve energy efficiency.

candidate_dps: DP1: Deploy energy and tonnage sensors with real-time telemetry reporting.
DP2: Implement adaptive vehicle control algorithms to reduce energy consumption.
DP3: Use data analytics and scheduling optimization modules to plan efficient operations.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, and DP3 combined to address monitoring, control, and optimization respectively.

potential_sources: Data dependency between monitoring and control modules.
Feedback loop from optimization to control parameters.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is expected and manageable with clear interface definitions and modular design.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate and reliable sensor data.
Risk of control algorithm complexity impacting real-time responsiveness.
Optimization effectiveness depends on quality of input data and models.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider modularizing optimization separately to allow iterative improvements without impacting control logic.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical components and embedded control systems responsible for sensing and controlling energy consumption.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle sends real-time energy and tonnage data to NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle receives operational commands and control parameters from NGHE Solution.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of sensing and control logic; low diffusion as control algorithms are embedded.
Contains optimization and analytics; moderate diffusion due to data exchange.
Minimal containment; mainly remote control and monitoring.
No direct containment; interfaces mainly for safety alerts.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy monitoring and control are best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize interface complexity and latency.
containment_risks: Potential difficulty in updating control algorithms embedded in Vehicle hardware/software.
Risk of insufficient integration with NGHE Solution optimization if interfaces are not well defined.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts between Vehicle and NGHE Solution for control parameters and telemetry data.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well matched by the selected DPs, covering monitoring, control, and optimization aspects.

diffusion_risks: Data exchange between Vehicle and NGHE Solution may cause latency or synchronization issues.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment within Vehicle for sensing/control and NGHE Solution for optimization is appropriate and aligns with module capabilities.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring energy_per_ton and validating telemetry and control effectiveness through system tests.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of monitoring and control to Vehicle, optimization to NGHE Solution, with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Enhance interface definitions between Vehicle and NGHE Solution to mitigate diffusion risks.
Consider modular updates for control algorithms to reduce embedded system update risks.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, interface definitions and update strategies need refinement to reduce risks and improve modularity.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved in real-time.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize operational parameters and task scheduling to improve energy efficiency.

revise.why: To refine interface contracts and update mechanisms between Vehicle and NGHE Solution modules.

assumptions: Sensor data is accurate and timely.
Vehicle embedded systems can support adaptive control algorithms.
open_risks: Latency and synchronization issues in telemetry and control data exchange.
Difficulty in updating embedded control software.
evidence_needed: Performance data from prototype Vehicle telemetry and control systems.
Interface specification documents between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.
